characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The patriarch of the Winslow family and an officer for the Chicago Police Department. Acrofatic: Carl's remarkably fast, despite his spare tire. Just look at any occasion when he chases Steve. Action Dad: Justified since he's a cop. He has taken down crooks and has verbally defended his family (and Steve) several times and helped to defuse a bomb. Adapted Out: He along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Angrish: More than once, usually because of Steve's latest disaster.

characters.name: Carl Winslow
characters.description: The patriarch of the Winslow family and an officer for the Chicago Police Department. Acrofatic: Carl's remarkably fast, despite his spare tire. Just look at any occasion when he chases Steve. Action Dad: Justified since he's a cop. He has taken down crooks and has verbally defended his family (and Steve) several times and helped to defuse a bomb. Adapted Out: He along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Angrish: More than once, usually because of Steve's latest disaster.

characters.name: Harriette Winslow
characters.description: Carl's strong-willed wife and the mother of Eddie, Laura, and Judy. Adapted Out: She along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. The Artifact: After Steve became the undisputed main character, more and more members of the Winslow family got phased out. Unlike Laura, Eddie, and Carl, Harriette lacked any dynamic and comedic relationship with Steve leaving her as the odd man out. Ascended Extra: Prior to being one of the main characters of this series, she was a recurring character inPerfect Strangers. Beware the Nice Ones: While Harriette is a nice person, she has no problem telling somebody off, that includes family too.

characters.name: Estelle Winslow
characters.description: Carl's mother who is a wiseCool Old Lady. Adapted Out: She along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Advertised Extra: In the later seasons, she's entirely absent in many episodes and is rarely important to the plot, despite being the third billed character in the opening for most of the show. Beware the Nice Ones: Estelle may be a kind old lady, but she is more than capable of taking care of herself. For example, in "Stake-Out", she throws a criminal on the ground when he tries to attack her. Catchphrase:"Way to go, Carl!"

characters.name: Eddie Winslow
characters.description: The oldest child, he's an athletic and rebellious teenager. Adapted Out: He along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Big Brother Instinct:He occasionally has this relationship with Laura. When Ted, one of her boyfriends,started a rumor that they slept together, Eddie angrily grabbed him and pinned him to the wall before threatening him into admitting what actually happened.This can describe his friendship with Steve. As much as Steve annoys him, Eddie will always be there for him when push comes to shove. Even when a frat house, that he really wanted to be apart of, told him that either he stops being friends with Steve or lose his chance at being accepted, Eddie chose Steve in a heartbeat. He occasionally has this relationship with Laura. When Ted, one of her boyfriends,started a rumor that they slept together, Eddie angrily grabbed him and pinned him to the wall before threatening him into admitting what actually happened. This can describe his friendship with Steve. As much as Steve annoys him, Eddie will always be there for him when push comes to shove. Even when a frat house, that he really wanted to be apart of, told him that either he stops being friends with Steve or lose his chance at being accepted, Eddie chose Steve in a heartbeat.

characters.name: Laura Winslow
characters.description: The older daughter, she's smart, popular, and the dream girl of Steve Urkel. The Ace: In the first season, she's just a brilliant student. As the show goes on, she becomes aDude Magnet, head cheerleader, andSchool Idol, while still being a brilliant student. Adapted Out: She along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Played with. Almost all of her potential suitors who serve asRomantic False Leadsare unlikable jerks but this mostly happens because she's aHorrible Judge of Character. When she realizes that they are jerks, she loses interest in them. She also turns down the advances of bad boys and bullies several times (like Willie or Weasel). All Guys Want Cheerleaders: Laura is a cheerleader and an insanely popularDude Magnet.

characters.name: Judy Winslow
characters.description: The youngest cute and sassy daughter of the Winslows who disappears after Season 4. Advertised Extra: As a member of the main family, she's credited as a main character in the first four seasons. While she’s in nearly every episode of Seasons 1 and 2, she's usually used as a background character who is lucky if she gets a line in nearly every episode by Season 3. In her final season, before her unexplained disappearance, she doesn't evenappearin most episodes, despite still being in the opening credits. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: One of the most infamous examples of this. She disappears after Season 4, without explanation. Afterwards, the other characters act as if she never existed and Harriette and Carl only had two kids (Eddie and Laura). A Day in the Limelight:Downplayedas Judy never really gets a day in the limelight, only a few subplots:In "Fast Eddie Winslow", she must write a book report aboutSwiss Family Robinsonafter she doesn't read the book.In "Ice Station Winslow", she feels ignored by her family because of her younger cousin Richie.In "A Thought in the Dark", she ruins Harriette's dress and begs Carl to cover for her.noteAnd despite being one of the few Judy subplots, she only appears in one scene.In "Taking Credit", she's in theFraming Devicealong with Rachel and Richie. In "Fast Eddie Winslow", she must write a book report aboutSwiss Family Robinsonafter she doesn't read the book.

characters.name: Richie Crawford
characters.description: The youngest member of the family and Rachel's only son. Adapted Out: He along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Advertised Extra: Despite being a member of the family and credited as a main character in Seasons 1-7, he is entirely absent in many episodes, especially from Season 4 onwards, and rarely important to the plot, save for the occasionalA Day in the Limelight. That said, he gets much more focus than Judy and unlike her, he keeps appearing sporadically until the end of the series. The Baby of the Bunch: The youngest of the family who's literally a baby in Season 1. Big Brother Worship: He worships Steve as another brother, making him the only other Winslow besides Estelle who enjoys Urkel's company.

characters.name: Rachel Crawford
characters.description: Harriette's attractive and widowed younger sister. Adapted Out: She along with the rest of the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. And Starring: "And Telma Hopkins as Rachel". Commuting on a Bus: She makes occasional guest appearances in Season 6 and one final appearance in Season 9. Cool Aunt: For the Winslow kids.

characters.name: Steve Urkel
characters.description: The show'sBreakout Character, he's the smart, clumsy, and nerdy neighbor of the Winslows. Abhorrent Admirer: His undying devotion to Laura is not received well at all. But that has just as much (or more) to do with his outrageously weird personality than the way he looks. Abusive Parents:His parentsare often implied to be this. Examples include trying to push him back into the womb, not owning a car because he was born in one, giving him a curfew that doesn't dictate when hemustcome home, but when he'sallowedto, neglecting to feed him, making him pay rent even as a child, and finallymoving to Russia without him. Of course, with his parents gone, Steve moves in with the Winslows, whom he likes better. By his own admission:Steve:Carl, I love you like a father and my father like a... neighbor. Academic Athlete: Downplayed. He's a brilliant student andcan play basketball, but he's not so good at other athletic activities. Accidental Pervert: Walks in on Laura in the shower by mistake when both planned to take one at the same time.

characters.name: Waldo Geraldo Faldo
characters.description: Eddie's best friend, known for not being the brightest bulb. Adapted Out: He doesn't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Ascended Extra: Shawn Harrison first appeared in the Season 1 episode "The Party", credited as "Guy in Towel." Season 2 was when Waldo got his first proper appearance, portrayed as a sidekick to a bully, playing this role in two episodes. Eventually, Waldo returned, and was re-written as Eddie's best friend, and joined the main cast by Season 4. Beta Couple: With Maxine. Bumbling Sidekick: Initially, he was this to Willie, the school bully and a minor villain. Starting in Season 3, he becomes this to Eddie.

characters.name: Myra Monkhouse
characters.description: A pretty and bubbly girl who is madly in love with Steve. Abhorrent Admirer: Zigzagged. Steve is attracted to Myra because of her beauty and intelligence, but at the same time, he's creeped outby her stalkerish and possessive ways. This is mostly prominent in season 5, when Steve still has feelings for Laura, and he doesn't know what to do with Myra's obsession with him. She grows out of it in seasons 6-8, when she has a real relationship with Steve, but then it's brought back in the final season after their break-up, even worse than before. Adapted Out: She doesn't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Alliterative Name:MyraMonkhouse. Ascended Extra: She's introduced as a cousin of Ted, Laura's boyfriend at the time. Like Steve, she was supposed to be a one shot character but eventually becomes part of the main cast and gets aPromotion to Opening Titles, even if her cousin Ted disappears after a few episodes.

characters.name: Jerry Jamal " 3 J" Jameson
characters.description: A young orphan adopted by Carl and Harriette in the last few seasons. Adapted Out: He along with the Winslows don't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Alliterative Name: Jerry Jamal Jameson. Cousin Oliver: He's introduced in Season 7. Ironically, Richie's still around when this occurs. Foil: 3J finds some of Richie's pastimes to be boring, and is more morally ambiguous than he, but the two ultimately become best friends.

characters.name: Rodney Beckett
characters.description: Eddie's best friend in the first two seasons. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: He is last seen in "Fast Eddie Winslow", then is mentioned once again by Eddie in "Taking Credit". It's not clear what happened to him from Season 3 onwards. Everyone Has Standards: He dislikes Steve, but he supports Eddie when the latter protects Steve from a bully in "In a Jam". Out of Focus: Most of his appearances are in the first season. In Season 2, he only appears in two episodes. The Prankster: As seen in his first episode, he likes practical jokes.

characters.name: Maxine Johnson
characters.description: Adapted Out: She doesn't appear inUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. All Girls Want Bad Boys: Before Waldo, she dated several jerks in the early seasons. Beta Couple: With Waldo for most of the series. Book Dumb: Unlike Laura, she gets poor grades. Celebrity Paradox: Max had aPunky Brewsterlunchbox as a kid, a show that also featured her actress, Cherie Johnson. Doubles as anActor Allusion.

characters.name: Lieutenant Murtaugh
characters.description: Carl's obnoxious boss in early seasons. Abhorrent Admirer: To Rachel, who's repulsed by him just as much as Laura is repulsed by Steve. He and Steve even bond over their shared dilemma in one episode. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: He disappears without a mention and is never heard from again after his last appearance. The Comically Serious: Whenever he isn't playing theJerkass, particularly in his first few appearances. He often says ridiculous things with a serious face. Commonality Connection: He bonds with Steve overAll Love Is Unrequited.

characters.name: Cassie Lynn Nubbles
characters.description: Laura and Steve's snobby nemesis from high school in Season 3. Alpha Bitch: Her entire existence seems to involve causing problems for both Laura and Steve. Blackmail: What Cassie tries to pull on Laura in "Woman of the People", when the former made unflattering snapshots of both her and Steve embracing to make her drop out of the race. Steve, with the help of Eddie, turns the tables on Cassie by kissing her and threatens to go public with his photos. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: She disappears after losing the student body campaign in Season 3. Dumb Blonde: She's not the brightest young student Vanderbilt High has to offer. In "Food, Lies, and Videotape", she treats a Home Economics course as an easy pass to get back on the cheerleading squad. Ms. Steuben lampshades that her class is not aMickey Mousecourse and humbles Cassie by shoving her hand into the dough, much to the latter's embarrassment.

characters.name: Ted Curran
characters.description: Laura's boyfriend in Season 4. The Ace: He's described as a "good-looking, popular jockwith straight A's". Big Man on Campus: He's as popular as Laura, and it's the reason why she has a crush on him. Lovable Jock: Despite his mistakes, he is one of Laurs's nicer love interests. Not So Above It All: His friends spread fake rumors about him and Laura sleeping together, and he does nothing to stop the rumors, causing Laura, Eddie, and Steve to be mad at him. He later feels guilty.

characters.name: Alex "Weasel" Park
characters.description: Eddie's obnoxious friend from school. Originally one of Ted's buddies, he would eventually start hanging around Eddie and Waldo. Abhorrent Admirer: He flirts with Laura in more than one episode. She doesn't take it well. Animal Motifs: A weasel, obviously. He's a smug, preeningJerkasswho isn't nearly as smart as he thinks he is. Characterization Marches On: Like Waldo, he was originally a crony of an antagonistic character in his debut episode before being retooled into being one of Eddie's friends. In this case, he was one of Ted's friends. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: After being a recurring character in Seasons 4 and 5, he disappears for the rest of the series.

characters.name: Myrtle Urkel
characters.description: Steve's cousin who is madly in love with Eddie. Abhorrent Admirer: She romantically pursues Eddie, who finds her unattractive and obnoxious. Ascended Extra: She first appears in the Season 2 episode "Cousin Urkel". After several years, she comes back as a recurring character. Character Development: After one too many times of rejection, Myrtle has had enough and actually gives up on Eddie and moves on. Clingy Jealous Girl: She doesnottake kindly to other women being around her beloved Eddie.

characters.name: GretaMcClure
characters.description: Eddie's girlfriend in later seasons. Calling the Old Man Out: She reproaches her father Dave for his overprotectiveness, saying that it's pushing her away. This is initially enough for Dave to relent and let her date Eddie, but his overprotectiveness makes Greta estranged from him. Dating What Daddy Hates:Her overprotective father doesn't like Eddie. It gets to the point where her father threatens to financially cut her off unless she quits dating Eddie. She refuses and has to find her own income.When Greta and Eddie use Steve as a ringer to pick Greta up for their date, her father actually approves of him. When Greta asks why, her father points out that she could probably beat the crap out of Steve if he actually tried anything.When she refuses to break up with Eddie, her father not only disowned her but left her to be cut off financially. Her overprotective father doesn't like Eddie. It gets to the point where her father threatens to financially cut her off unless she quits dating Eddie. She refuses and has to find her own income. When Greta and Eddie use Steve as a ringer to pick Greta up for their date, her father actually approves of him. When Greta asks why, her father points out that she could probably beat the crap out of Steve if he actually tried anything.

characters.name: Curtis Williams
characters.description: Laura's boyfriend in Seasons 7 and 8. Did Not Get the Girl: Ends up losing Laura to Stefan in one of his final appearances. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: More of a jerk in his first episode, but becomes more likable in later appearances. Love Interest: To Laura, and later to Maxine after Laura breaks up with him. Troubled, but Cute: A tough, reckless car driver with a "bad boy" appeal, and Laura finds him attractive.

characters.name: Mr. Looney
characters.description: The meek and slightly eccentric janitor at Vanderbilt High. He's good friends with Steve Urkel. Ascended Extra: He first appears in "Presumed Urkel" in a minor role, but would later become a prominent character in two more episodes. Catchphrase: "It's pronounced Loo-NAY, it's French!" A Day in the Limelight: "Wedding Bell Blues" gives him a lot of focus, since it's about his big (and soon to be failed) wedding day. Driven to Suicide: After hisbride-to-be abandons him at the altar, a grief-stricken Looney climbs to the top of the church and nearly hurls himself over the edge. Thankfully, Steve and Carl are able to intervene.

characters.name: Nick Neidermeyer
characters.description: The obnoxious, womanizing neighbor who antagonizes the Winslows in season 7 and 8. Alliterative Name:NickNeidermeyer. Arc Villain: Serves as one for both Carl and Harriette. Ascended Extra: He is introduced as Harriette's boss from Ferguson Department Stores in the episode "Bugged", but later returns in "Dream Date" as the Winslow family's next door neighbor. Batman Gambit: In "Karate Kids", he attempted to sue the Winslows upon slipping on a toy truck, but his plan blew up in his face after Harriette exposed him faking his neck injuries the whole time.

characters.name: Herb and Diane Urkel
characters.description: The unseen parents of Steve Urkel. They implicitly despise their son, and do everything they can to avoid him at all costs. Abusive Parents: They've been abusive towards Steve ever since he wasborn, as Herb apparently tried to shove him back into the womb the moment he saw his bizarre appearance. Steve later mentions that they once gave him exploding trick candles on his birthday, and have attempted to outright abandon him at one point. The Faceless: Diane is very briefly seen in one of Steve's baby pictures, but her face is obscured by her son's comically-large head. The Ghost: Unlike Diane, who isThe FacelessandThe Voice, we never see or hear Dr. Urkel in any capacity. All we know is that he is "a teeny, tiny man". Hollywood Atheist: Dr. Urkel thinks that church is a bunch of nonsense, and doesn't understand why his son would believe in God despite his brilliant, scientific mind. Steve argues that God is like an atom: he can't feel or touch an atom, yet he believes that it exists.

characters.name: Aunt Oona
characters.description: Steve's aunt from Altoona. She's very shy and introverted, but later proves herself to be quite the fabulous singer. Actor Allusion: Donna Summer gets to sing her own songs in-character. She sings "Last Dance" for the karaoke contest, and "She Works Hard for the Money" in her final appearance. Apologises a Lot: She actually manages to driveStevecrazy by following him around to apologizeforfollowing him around. Big Beautiful Woman: She gains quite a bit of weight in her second episode, due to her new job at The Ham Hut. The local pastor quickly falls for her, and even outright states thathe loves big, beautiful women. Black and Nerdy: Just like her nephew. And she's played by one of the greatest Black singers in disco, to boot.

characters.name: Stefan Urquelle
characters.description: Steve's charismaticSexier Alter Ego. The Ace: He's talented and good at everything. Unlike Steve, he also has social skills and is loved by everyone. Ascended Extra: He makes a single appearance in the Season 5 episode "Dr. Urkel and Mr. Cool", when Steve changes his DNA as a plan to become cool. Later, he turns into Stefan several times and in Season 7, thanks to Steve's cloning machine, Steve and Stefan become two different characters. Blessed with Suck: His talents and charms come with the drawback that his intellect is not on the same genius level as Steve's. Thus, when Myra messes with the transformation chamber and ends up turning Carl into a nerdy "Urkelized" version of himself, Stefan has trouble fixing the machine. Born as an Adult: Well, he's Steve's clone (originally alter ego).

characters.name: Bruce Lee Urkel
characters.description: When a gang of bar thugs harass him and Laura, Steve concocts a special elixir mixed with the DNA ofBruce Lee, turning him into a formidable martial arts master with Lee's appearance and skills. In this powerful new form, Steve is able to combat hordes of bad guys with kung fu. Bar Brawl: His first fight as Bruce Lee takes place in a rowdy bar full of thugs. He wipes the floor with every single one of them. Battle Cry: Jumps into combat with Lee's signature "WA-TAH!" Beware the Nice Ones: Even in this form, Steve is quite affable with his enemies, but the moment they pick a fight with him, he unleashes hell. Bruce Lee Clone: "Bruce Juice", a transformation elixir mixed with Bruce Lee's DNA, turns Steve into the mighty Bruce Lee Urkel, who retains Lee's signature hairstyle, accent, and martial arts skills. The three episodes that feature him directly homage Lee's filmography.

characters.name: Urkelbot
characters.description: A highly-intelligent robot built by Steve. He shares all of his creator's traitsincludinghis love for Laura, and goes absolutely haywire trying to pursue her. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: The moment he meets Laura, Urkelbot becomes an obsessiveYanderewho’s willing to betray his own creator just to win her affection. Beware the Silly Ones: You can't get much sillier than a robot based on Steve, but Urkelbot still hones remarkable strength and later becomes a skilled fighter. Breakout Character: So far, he is the only character who has appeared alongside Steve in other media, playing a supporting role in animated projects likeScooby-Doo and Guess Who?andUrkel Saves Santa: The Movie!. Distaff Counterpart: At the end of "Robo-Nerd II", Steve builds Urkelbot a girlfriend, "Laurabot".

characters.name: Stevil
characters.description: A creepy ventriloquist dummy that Steve brings home on Halloween night. During one terrifying nightmare, he springs to life to haunt Steve and the Winslows. Antagonist Title: His Halloween specials are titled "Stevil" and "Stevil II: This Time He's Not Alone" respectively. Appropriated Appellation: Steve calling him evil is what inspires him to name himself "Stevil". The Bad Guy Wins: "Stevil II: This Time He's Not Alone" ends with him successfully stealing both Steve and Carl's souls. Thankfully,it's only a nightmare. Big Bad: Of the "Stevil" duology. In the sequel, heteams up with another dummy based on Carl, named Carlsbad.
